threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'tube'. Locals just hop on the 'overground' like it’s the only option, eyes rolling at your touristy lingo.
Caught a night bus from Peckham and overheard a bloke raving about how he saw a pigeon steal fries from a toddler. Classic London!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I heard a neighbor playing accordion while waiting for the bus, and another joined in with a song. That’s Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you get a kebab at 3 AM after a night out, and the vendor still remembers your order from last week.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they know the best taco truck isn’t La Taqueria, but the one tucked behind the park at 24th.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in full tuxedo, tails and all, ride the 47 home after a wedding. That’s classic SF—where glamour meets grit on the same bus.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists never notice the small streets in Gràcia are alive at night, but only when the locals gather for their secret barbecues on rooftops.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by the way they still stare in awe at the Casa Batlló while everyone else just rushes by.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a 'left turn' means going the correct way down a one-way street. Better learn the shortcuts before your cycle dodges get old.
Last night on the 196, a guy serenaded the entire bus with acoustic covers. Strange mix of applause and eye rolls when he hit a dodgy note.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a whole group doing yoga in the middle of the street during a spontaneous block party, I thought, yep, this is home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can literally hop on a tram with a beer in hand here. Just don’t forget to keep it discreet, or you'll catch some serious side-eye.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local by their confidence in navigating BART chaos—if they’re calmly checking the app for delays instead of panicking, they’re legit.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy with an accordion serenading Muni riders at the Civic Center stop, completely oblivious to the chaos from the BART delays. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think we all know each other here, but with so many new residents, it feels more like a patchwork than a community.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them easily; they still carry a mapa de papel, struggling to navigate the winding streets while locals zoom past on scooters.
